# Cost Forecast — 2026-06-18

**Subscription:** Azure subscription 1 (`ae5e40cd-96ef-48d8-950a-2e22cf8f991a`)
**Currency:** GBP
**Baseline window:** 2026-06-15 → 2026-06-17 (3 full post-cutover days) + 14-day component run-rates
**Generated by:** /cost-forecast

---

## Headline

All windows start today (2026-06-18) to match Azure's forecast API windows. Bottom-up = ~£1.807/day clean steady-state run-rate × days in window.

| Horizon          | Window              | Bottom-up forecast | Azure forecast | Delta              | Confidence |
|------------------|---------------------|-------------------:|---------------:|-------------------:|------------|
| Rest of month    | 06-18 → 06-30 (13d) | £23.49             | £15.50         | **+£7.99 (+52%)**  | High       |
| Next 30 days     | 06-18 → 07-17 (30d) | £54.21             | £35.44         | **+£18.77 (+53%)** | Medium     |
| Next quarter     | 06-18 → 09-16 (90d) | £162.63            | £120.28        | **+£42.35 (+35%)** | Low        |

**TL;DR — Azure still *under*-forecasts by 35–53%, same as yesterday, for the same reason.** The mid-June go-live step-change (warm Go prod replica + active Service Bus polling + rising Cosmos RU) is now three clean days old (06-15/16/17 at £1.82/£1.81/£1.79), and the run-rate has settled at **~£1.81/day**. Azure's trailing-average model is still dragged down by the cheap first half of June (~£1.0–1.1/day, pre-cutover scale-to-zero) and lands ~£1.19/day — roughly a third too low. **No cost-relevant infra change shipped since yesterday's report** (the only `/infra` commit, `a31923b3`, was a zero-diff dashboard refactor — Pulumi preview reported "32 unchanged"), so the forecast is a re-confirmation with one extra day of evidence. Budget for **~£54/next-30-days**, not Azure's ~£35. Calendar-month June will land around **£47–48** (the cheap pre-cutover first half pulls the whole-month figure below the current run-rate).

---

## Why Azure's forecast is wrong

Unchanged drivers from 2026-06-17 — now confirmed with a third clean day of actuals:

- **Go-live warm replica — the main driver Azure misses (+£0.25/day, ~£7.5/mo, always-on).** `ca-town-crier-api-go-prod` runs `minReplicas=1` (warm) and has cost a steady **£0.248/day** across 06-15→17. The .NET prod app it replaced was scale-to-zero and cost **£0 on idle days** (June 1–9 show zero Container Apps spend). A continuous cost appeared mid-month that did not exist in the trailing window Azure averages over. Deliberate, accepted go-live decision — *not* flagged for reversal (see Recommendations).

- **Cosmos is still drifting up (+£0.25/day over the month).** `cosmos-town-crier-shared` ran **£1.234 / £1.282 / £1.231** on 06-15/16/17 (3-day mean **£1.249/day**), up from ~£1.00/day in early June. Azure's trailing 30-day average (~£0.98/day) sits below the *current* level and keeps falling behind as the line drifts up. Cosmos is **~69%** of the bill.

- **Active polling is continuous (+£0.10/day).** `job-tc-poll-prod` holds a steady **£0.098/day** (negligible in early June). Event-triggered Service Bus polling is now busy every cycle.

- **The .NET → Go cutover overlap (one-off, now fully gone).** Old `ca-town-crier-api-prod`/`-dev` last billed on **06-14** (£0.165 / £0.226) and are **£0 from 06-15 onward** — confirmed in the actuals. They added a one-off ~£1.6 to June MTD (06-10→14 double-running) and are correctly **excluded** from the forward run-rate. Azure still extrapolates a sliver of that overlap forward, partially offsetting (but not cancelling) the under-forecast.

- **Verified change-points in the actuals timeline:**

  | Date       | Commit / event                                       | Effect on run-rate                                          |
  |------------|------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|
  | 2026-06-10 | `8120a3f9` Go dev app added; cutover testing begins  | Container Apps line wakes from £0; .NET+Go overlap starts   |
  | 2026-06-14 | `621714c2` Go prod bound + warmed; `.NET` apps stop  | Cutover peak £2.20/day (one-off); .NET → £0 from 06-15      |
  | 2026-06-15 | First clean Go-only steady state                     | £1.82/day (06-15), £1.81 (06-16), £1.79 (06-17)             |
  | 2026-06-18 | `a31923b3` dashboard refactor                        | **Zero cost impact** — Pulumi preview "32 unchanged"        |
  | (ongoing)  | Cosmos RU drift                                      | £1.00 → £1.25/day over June                                 |

## Spend by service (last 30 days)

_Period: 2026-05-20 → 2026-06-18 vs prior period 2026-04-19 → 2026-05-19._

| Service              | Last 30d   | Prior 30d  | Δ vs prior |
|----------------------|-----------:|-----------:|-----------:|
| Azure Cosmos DB      | £29.33     | £30.47     | −£1.14     |
| Azure Container Apps | £4.63      | £2.62      | **+£2.01** |
| Container Registry   | £3.67      | £3.90      | −£0.23     |
| Azure Monitor        | £0.11      | £0.03      | +£0.08     |
| Email (ACS)          | £0.02      | £0.02      | £0.00      |
| Service Bus          | £0.00      | £0.00      | £0.00      |
| Log Analytics        | £0.00      | £0.50      | −£0.50     |
| **Total**            | **£37.76** | **£37.55** | **+£0.21** |

> ⚠️ **The two 30-day totals being roughly equal is the trap.** Both *average* ~£1.25/day, but the last-30d window blends cheap pre-go-live days (~£1.0/day) with the new ~£1.8/day run-rate. The **exit rate** (mid-June, £1.81/day) is well above either window's mean — exactly why a trailing-window forecast under-shoots. The visible mover is **Container Apps +£2.01** (warm Go prod replica + dev deploy churn + the .NET/Go overlap one-off landing inside this window). Cosmos still dominates at **~78%** of the 30-day spend.

| Resource                      | Service              | Daily run-rate | Rest-of-month | Notes                                                                       |
|-------------------------------|----------------------|---------------:|--------------:|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| cosmos-town-crier-shared      | Azure Cosmos DB      | £1.249         | £16.24        | ~50 RU/s and still drifting up. Serverless. ~69% of the bill. See rec. tc-tm5o. |
| ca-town-crier-api-go-prod     | Azure Container Apps | £0.248         | £3.22         | **Warm** (`minReplicas=1`). Always-on cost since go-live.                    |
| acrtowncriershared            | Container Registry   | £0.124         | £1.61         | Fixed Basic SKU daily fee. 71 MB / 10 GB — no overage.                       |
| job-tc-poll-prod              | Azure Container Apps | £0.098         | £1.27         | Event-triggered Service Bus polling; continuously active.                    |
| ca-town-crier-api-go-dev      | Azure Container Apps | £0.059         | £0.77         | Scale-to-zero (`minReplicas=0`); cost is deploy churn. 43 active revisions mid-week, reset by the weekly cleanup job → tc-fxau. |
| job-tc-poll-bootstrap-prod    | Azure Container Apps | £0.009         | £0.12         | 30-min schedule; no-op when queue healthy.                                   |
| job-tc-digest-hourly-prod     | Azure Container Apps | £0.006         | £0.08         | Hourly cron.                                                                 |
| job-tc-digest-hourly-dev      | Azure Container Apps | £0.006         | £0.08         | Hourly cron.                                                                 |
| log-town-crier-shared         | Log Analytics        | £0.004         | £0.05         | Near-zero. Basic-Logs + 30d + 1 GB cap. Within free grant.                   |
| job-tc-dormant-cleanup-prod   | Azure Container Apps | £0.003         | £0.04         | Daily cron.                                                                  |
| acs-town-crier-uk             | Email                | £0.001         | £0.01         | Minimal email send cost.                                                     |
| other near-zero jobs + SB     | mixed                | £0.000         | £0.00         | digest-prod/dev, dormant-dev, sb-town-crier-prod — all ~£0.                  |
| **Total**                     |                      | **£1.807**     | **£23.49**    | 13 forward days (06-18 → 06-30) at run-rate.                                 |

**Excluded from the forward run-rate** (present in June MTD but decommissioned): `ca-town-crier-api-prod` (.NET, £0 from 06-15), `ca-town-crier-api-dev` (.NET, £0 from 06-15), `acs-town-crier-shared` (legacy Europe ACS, deleted 05-02), `job-tc-poll-safety-net-prod`, `job-tc-poll-dev` (renamed/removed in April). Both Static Web Apps (`swa-town-crier-dev`/`-prod`) are **Free SKU — £0**.

---

## Reconciliation (Pulumi vs reality)

Reconciled against the live `az resource list` (29 resources across `rg-town-crier-dev`/`-prod`/`-shared`) as ground truth. `/infra` is Go-based as of 2026-06-17 (`221a84c5`, zero-diff); the 06-18 dashboard refactor (`a31923b3`) was also verified zero-diff. Resource graph unchanged from yesterday.

- **No drift / no orphans.** Every billed resource maps to a Town Crier resource group and a known purpose. No portal-created or leftover resources.
- **Untagged (5):** two managed certificates (`cert-api-dev`/`-prod`) and three Azure-auto-created monitor objects (Smart Detection action group, `azureapp-auto` action group, Service Health activity-log alert). All system-generated and free — expected, no action.
- **Revision accumulation on dev — bounded by an existing weekly cleanup.** `ca-town-crier-api-go-dev` (`activeRevisionsMode=Multiple`) ran 22 active revisions (06-17) → 38 (06-18) → 43 (06-19). This is **not** an unbounded climb: `.github/workflows/dev-container-app-cleanup.yml` runs weekly (Mon 03:00 UTC) and deactivates all-but-newest active revisions (last run 2026-06-15 succeeded), `infra/environment.go` sets `MaxInactiveRevisions=5`, and prod runs `Single` (1 revision, Azure auto-deactivates). The Monday job resets dev to 1–2 active. The real (smaller) gap: the inline per-deploy deactivation in `.github/actions/deploy-container-app/action.yml` keys off traffic `weight==100`, which returns empty for dev's `latestRevision:true` traffic, so it no-ops on every deploy — leaving the weekly job as the *sole* brake. ~9 revisions/day accumulate; a high-PR-volume week could approach the 100 cap mid-week. → **tc-fxau (P3, reframed to the inline-deactivation bug).**
- **Prior fixes still holding:** ACR is at **71 MB / 10 GB** (no overage; the April purge stuck). App* tables remain at 30-day retention. No regression on either.

---

## Recommendations

No new recommendations this cycle — nothing cost-relevant shipped since 2026-06-17. The two open beads carry forward; `tc-fxau` is reframed to its actual root cause (see correction note in §2).

### 1. Evaluate Cosmos cost reduction (throughput model + write efficiency)

- **Estimated saving:** ~£10–20/month (uncertain; carries throttling risk)
- **Bead:** `tc-tm5o` (open, P3)
- **Rationale:** Cosmos is **~69–78% of the bill** (~£30/mo of the last-30d £37.76) at ~50 RU/s and still drifting up, driven mainly by polling upserts against a pre-revenue user base. Two levers: **(a)** a database-level **shared autoscale** throughput (e.g. 1000 RU/s max) could plausibly land ~£10–15/mo since average usage sits near the 10% autoscale floor — but needs a polling-burst profile first (spikes above the cap throttle, and the app has previously fought serverless 429s) and a check that all containers can share throughput; **(b)** ~50 RU/s *continuous* with no real users implies redundant re-upserts of unchanged planning records each poll cycle — cutting that demand is the more durable fix. Quantified-but-uncertain and risk-bearing against the go-live reliability posture, hence P3.

### 2. Fix the inline per-deploy revision deactivation for dev (Multiple mode)

- **Estimated saving:** ~£0/month (robustness, not cost)
- **Bead:** `tc-fxau` (open, P3 — reframed; the earlier P2 "near-term outage" framing was **wrong** and is corrected below)
- **Rationale:** Revision cleanup is already wired and working — prod is `Single` mode (auto-deactivates), `dev-container-app-cleanup.yml` runs weekly (Mon 03:00 UTC, last run 06-15 succeeded), and `MaxInactiveRevisions=5` is set in infra. The dev count (43 mid-week) is normal accumulation the Monday job resets, **not** a runaway. The genuine gap is narrower: `deploy-container-app`'s inline "deactivate old revision" step looks up the old revision via traffic `weight==100`, which is empty for dev's `latestRevision:true` traffic, so it silently no-ops every deploy (`|| true` hides it too). That leaves the weekly job as the sole brake instead of a safety net, so a high-PR week could brush the 100 cap mid-week. Fix: capture the newest-*active* revision before deploy and deactivate it after (mirrors the weekly job inline). Dev-only.

### Noted but deliberately NOT recommended

- **Warm Go prod replica (`minReplicas=1`, ~£7.5/mo)** is the largest *controllable* line, but it is an accepted go-live decision to eliminate cold-start latency (ref `project_aca_min_replicas_decision`, set 2026-06-13). Reverting it would trade UX for ~£7.5/mo and contradicts the pre-revenue posture of prioritising reliability. Left as-is.
- **ACR Basic (£3.7/mo)**, **Service Bus Basic (£0)**, **Log Analytics (£0/mo)** are all already at their floors. No action.

---

## Methodology footnote

- **Actuals:** Cost Management query API, 60-day window (2026-04-19 → 2026-06-18), daily granularity, grouped by `ResourceId` (503 rows) and `ServiceName` (372 rows) via `scripts/fetch_costs.sh`. No pagination needed.
- **Run-rate:** per-resource mean over the 3 clean post-cutover days (06-15 → 06-17) — the only complete days with Go as the sole API and the .NET apps gone. 06-14 (cutover peak £2.20) excluded as a one-off; 06-18 excluded as a partial (incomplete same-day ingestion, £1.11 vs expected ~£1.81).
- **Azure forecast source:** Cost Management forecast API, Custom timeframe, three windows from 2026-06-18 (rest-of-month £15.50, next-30d £35.44, next-quarter £120.28). More internally consistent than yesterday's run, but still a naive trailing-average extrapolation that cannot fit the mid-June step-change.
- **Pulumi reconciliation:** done against `az resource list` ground truth (stack export not separately run; resource graph unchanged by the Go port and the zero-diff dashboard refactor). No drift identified.
- **ACR usage:** `az acr show-usage` — 71 MB used of the 10 GB Basic quota, no overage.
- **Exclusions / known gaps:** decommissioned .NET apps and renamed/removed April jobs excluded from forward projection (listed above). Next-quarter assumes the current run-rate plus continued mild Cosmos drift but **zero net-new user traffic** — real go-live adoption would push Cosmos (serverless) higher, so the £162.63 quarter figure is a **floor**, not a ceiling. Low confidence accordingly.
